meta.name: SLM World Registry
meta.description: The world's largest registry of Small Language Models under 8B parameters

aegis-nano.name: AegisNano
aegis-nano.description: A 6.3M-parameter transformer SLM purpose-built for detecting autonomous agent (bot) behavior vs human behavior from telemetry sequences. Trained via knowledge distillation from Llama 3.1 70B with behavioral tokenization encoding event types, inter-event timing, and jitter patterns into a 1024-token vocabulary. The smallest model in the SLM World registry — INT8 quantized footprint is just 6.5 MB, making it deployable on microcontrollers and edge devices. Detects three threat models: slow-burn agent fraud, agent hijacking via prompt injection, and synthetic biometric patterns. Also supports transfer learning for financial fraud detection.

falcon-7b-instruct.name: Falcon-7B-Instruct
falcon-7b-instruct.description: TII's Falcon-7B, one of the first truly open 7B-class models to compete with proprietary alternatives. Trained on 1.5T tokens of the RefinedWeb corpus. While surpassed by newer models in raw benchmarks, it remains a historically significant and fully Apache 2.0 licensed model for research and deployment.

gemma-2-2b-it.name: Gemma 2 2B IT
gemma-2-2b-it.description: Google's Gemma 2 at 2.6B parameters. Punching well above its weight class, this model delivers competitive performance against much larger models. Known for strong reasoning, clean outputs, and efficient deployment. Predecessor to the Gemma 3 4B, showing Google's progression in the small model space.

gemma-3-1b-it.name: Gemma 3 1B IT
gemma-3-1b-it.description: The smallest Gemma 3 variant at 1B parameters, supporting both text and image inputs. A remarkably capable tiny multimodal model that fits on virtually any device. Perfect for mobile vision-language tasks, on-device image understanding, and lightweight multimodal assistants.

gemma-3-4b-it.name: Gemma 3 4B IT
gemma-3-4b-it.description: A powerful 4B parameter multimodal instruction-tuned model from Google DeepMind supporting both text and image inputs. Achieves strong results on HumanEval (71.3%) and GSM8K (89.2%). Excels at code generation, math reasoning, and general chat tasks with the efficiency needed for edge deployment.

gemma-4-e2b-it.name: Gemma 4 E2B IT
gemma-4-e2b-it.description: Google DeepMind's most advanced sub-3B model supporting text, image, audio, and video inputs. Features Per-Layer Embeddings and hybrid attention architecture enabling it to run in under 1.5GB of memory. Purpose-built for on-device, mobile, and IoT deployment while maintaining strong multimodal reasoning capabilities.

granite-3-2-2b-instruct.name: Granite-3.2-2B-Instruct
granite-3-2-2b-instruct.description: IBM's enterprise-grade 2B parameter instruction-tuned model with native function calling capabilities and 128K context window. Optimized for enterprise tool use, structured data extraction, and production deployment. Combines small size with surprisingly strong performance on business automation tasks.

llama-3-2-3b-instruct.name: Llama 3.2 3B Instruct
llama-3-2-3b-instruct.description: Meta's widely-adopted 3B parameter instruction-tuned model with over 2.18M+ HuggingFace downloads. Features strong tool calling capabilities, structured JSON output, and a massive community ecosystem. Optimized for agentic pipelines, mobile apps, and community-supported projects. The Llama Community License permits most commercial use.

llama-3.2-1b-instruct.name: Llama 3.2 1B Instruct
llama-3.2-1b-instruct.description: Meta's smallest Llama 3.2 model at 1B parameters with 128K context. Optimized for mobile and on-device deployment with surprisingly strong instruction following and structured output. Pairs with Llama 3.2 3B as Meta's push into truly portable AI.

olmo-7b-instruct.name: OLMo-7B-Instruct
olmo-7b-instruct.description: A fully open 7B parameter instruction-tuned model from AI2, built on the principles of open science. All training data, code, weights, logs, and intermediate checkpoints are publicly available. Provides complete reproducibility and transparency unmatched by any other model in its class. Ideal for academic research and reproducible ML.

phi-3-mini-128k-instruct.name: Phi-3-mini-128k-instruct
phi-3-mini-128k-instruct.description: Long-context variant of Phi-3-mini with 128K token context window. Same strong reasoning capabilities as the 4K version but capable of processing entire books, lengthy documents, and extended conversations. MIT licensed. A strong choice for RAG alternatives and long-document QA.

phi-3-mini.name: Phi-3-mini-4k-instruct
phi-3-mini.description: A highly capable, lightweight 3.8 billion parameter model trained on a mix of heavily filtered web data and synthetic data, optimized for commercial and local use.

phi-3.5-mini-instruct.name: Phi-3.5-mini-instruct
phi-3.5-mini-instruct.description: The bridge between Phi-3 and Phi-4 at 3.8B parameters. Added 128K context window and significantly improved multilingual capabilities over Phi-3-mini. Retains the strong reasoning of the Phi family while enabling long-document processing and cross-lingual tasks. MIT licensed for maximum flexibility.

phi-4-mini-instruct.name: Phi-4-mini-instruct
phi-4-mini-instruct.description: Microsoft's best-in-class 3.8B parameter model trained on 5T tokens of high-quality filtered and synthetic data. Achieves top-tier reasoning benchmarks at its size: ARC-C 83.7%, GSM8K 88.6%. Extremely memory-efficient — runs in Q4 at just 2.49GB. Optimized for tight hardware, local deployment, and commercial use.

pythia-1.4b.name: Pythia-1.4B
pythia-1.4b.description: Part of EleutherAI's Pythia scaling suite — a set of 8 model sizes all trained on the same data in the same order. The 1.4B variant provides an excellent mid-size baseline for controlled experiments on model scaling, training dynamics, and interpretability. Apache 2.0 licensed with full training data available.

pythia-2.8b.name: Pythia-2.8B
pythia-2.8b.description: The 2.8B member of EleutherAI's Pythia scaling suite. Trained on the exact same data as all other Pythia models, enabling controlled experiments on how model capabilities emerge with scale. All 154 training checkpoints are available for studying training dynamics. Apache 2.0.

pythia-6.9b.name: Pythia-6.9B
pythia-6.9b.description: The largest model in EleutherAI's Pythia scaling suite at 6.9B parameters. Part of a controlled experiment across 8 model sizes (70M to 12B) all trained identically on The Pile. With 154 released checkpoints per model, Pythia is the gold standard for studying how language model capabilities develop during training.

qwen2-5-7b-instruct.name: Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct
qwen2-5-7b-instruct.description: A highly capable 7.6B parameter instruction-tuned model that sits at the top of the sub-8B range. Delivers exceptional performance across reasoning, code generation, and mathematical tasks. Supports 29+ languages and long-context processing up to 128K tokens. The strongest Apache 2.0 licensed model under 8B for general-purpose use.

qwen2.5-0.5b-instruct.name: Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct
qwen2.5-0.5b-instruct.description: The smallest member of the Qwen2.5 family at 0.5B parameters. Surprisingly capable for its size, supporting 29+ languages and 32K context. Ideal for resource-constrained environments, on-device deployment, and as a minimal baseline for fine-tuning experiments.

qwen2.5-1.5b-instruct.name: Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct
qwen2.5-1.5b-instruct.description: A compact 1.5B model from Alibaba's Qwen2.5 series. Balances strong performance with minimal resource requirements. Supports 29+ languages and delivers solid reasoning, chat, and instruction-following at a fraction of the cost of larger models.

qwen2.5-3b-instruct.name: Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct
qwen2.5-3b-instruct.description: The 3B variant of Qwen2.5 offering strong reasoning and code generation at a highly efficient size. Hits the sweet spot between TinyLlama-1.1B and 7B models for quality-per-parameter. Supports 29+ languages with solid instruction following.

qwen3-5-4b.name: Qwen3.5-4B
qwen3-5-4b.description: A state-of-the-art 4B parameter model with thinking/reasoning mode toggle, supporting 100+ languages. Features strong instruction following, long-document processing, and competitive performance across diverse benchmarks. Ideal for general-purpose deployment where quality and efficiency must be balanced.

smollm2-1.7b-instruct.name: SmolLM2-1.7B-Instruct
smollm2-1.7b-instruct.description: The largest SmolLM2 model at 1.7B parameters. Trained on the fully open smoltalk dataset and delivers strong reasoning and chat abilities. Complete transparency — weights, training data, and code are all publicly available. Excellent for research, fine-tuning, and deployment on consumer hardware.

smollm2-135m-instruct.name: SmolLM2-135M-Instruct
smollm2-135m-instruct.description: The smallest model in the SmolLM2 family at just 135M parameters. Remarkably capable for its size with basic instruction following and chat abilities. Fully open (weights + data + code). Ideal for embedded systems, research on tiny models, and extreme edge deployment.

smollm2-360m-instruct.name: SmolLM2-360M-Instruct
smollm2-360m-instruct.description: A 360M parameter instruction-tuned model from the SmolLM2 family. Provides a step up from the 135M variant with noticeably better reasoning and instruction following. Fully open (weights + data + code). Great for resource-constrained environments and research on small model capabilities.

smollm3-3b.name: SmolLM3-3B-Instruct
smollm3-3b.description: A fully open 3B parameter model from Hugging Face with dual-mode reasoning (thinking/no-think), structured tool calling (JSON/XML/Python), and support for 6 languages. Weights, training data, and code are all publicly available, making it the most transparent SLM in its class. Ideal for RAG systems, code assistants, and research.

tinyllama-1-1b.name: TinyLlama-1.1B-Chat-v1.0
tinyllama-1-1b.description: The gold standard for 1B-scale language models, trained on 3 trillion tokens with a Chinchilla-optimal approach. Despite its small size, it demonstrates surprisingly strong reasoning and conversational abilities. Ideal for research, resource-constrained environments, and as a baseline for fine-tuning experiments.

codefeedback-filtered-instruction.name: CodeFeedback-Filtered-Instruction
codefeedback-filtered-instruction.creator: m-a-p
codefeedback-filtered-instruction.description: A filtered and decontaminated code instruction dataset combining the best of Magicoder, ShareGPT code conversations, and Evol-Instruct code problems. 157K high-quality coding tasks with verified solutions across multiple programming languages. MIT licensed for unrestricted use.

conll2003.name: CoNLL-2003
conll2003.creator: CoNLL Shared Task
conll2003.description: The classic NER benchmark dataset. 20K sentences annotated with four entity types: PERSON, ORGANIZATION, LOCATION, and MISC. The standard evaluation set for named entity recognition in English. Simple, well-understood, and ideal for fine-tuning small models on NER.

drain3-behavioral.name: Drain3 Behavioral Telemetry Dataset
drain3-behavioral.creator: Aegis-Edge Team
drain3-behavioral.description: A synthetic behavioral telemetry dataset for training models to distinguish human from autonomous agent (bot) behavior. Contains 3,164 sequences tokenized from event logs (Android, OpenSSH, Linux, BGL) parsed through Drain3 log parser (22.47x compression ratio). Includes 3x data augmentation via synthetic jitter injection on anomaly examples. Features: event type IDs, delta-time bins, jitter bins encoded into a 1024-token vocabulary. Two classes: human sequences (exponential inter-event times, Rayleigh jitter) and bot sequences (constant timing, uniform low jitter). Designed for knowledge distillation training of small models.

gsm8k.name: GSM8K
gsm8k.creator: OpenAI
gsm8k.description: The standard benchmark for grade-school math word problems. 8.8K linguistically diverse problems requiring multi-step arithmetic reasoning. Each problem includes a step-by-step natural language solution. The de facto evaluation standard for math reasoning in language models.

lmsys-chat-1m.name: lmsys-chat-1m
lmsys-chat-1m.creator: LMSYS
lmsys-chat-1m.description: One million real user conversations with 25+ LLMs from Chatbot Arena. Contains diverse prompts, model responses, and human preference votes. Invaluable for understanding real-world usage patterns and training models to handle the full diversity of user requests.

magicoder-evol-instruct-110k.name: Magicoder-Evol-Instruct-110K
magicoder-evol-instruct-110k.creator: ise-uiuc
magicoder-evol-instruct-110k.description: A high-quality, decontaminated code instruction dataset with 110K programming problems and solutions across multiple languages. Generated using the Evol-Instruct method to progressively increase problem complexity. The MIT license makes it ideal for both research and commercial fine-tuning of code-focused SLMs.

metamathqa.name: MetaMathQA
metamathqa.creator: meta-math
metamathqa.description: 395K bootstrapped math questions generated by rewriting GSM8K and MATH problems from multiple perspectives. Trains models to approach the same problem through different reasoning paths. Proven effective at improving math performance in small models through data augmentation.

numina-math-cot.name: NuminaMath-CoT
numina-math-cot.creator: AI-MO (Numina)
numina-math-cot.description: Chain-of-thought math dataset from the team that won the AI Math Olympiad prize. 859K high-quality problems with detailed step-by-step solutions. Covers competition-level mathematics and advanced problem-solving strategies. The gold standard for training SLMs on mathematical reasoning.

oasst2.name: OpenAssistant Conversations (oasst2)
oasst2.creator: LAION
oasst2.description: Human-generated multi-turn conversation trees from the OpenAssistant project. 135K messages across 66K conversation trees in multiple languages. Each message is labeled for quality, making it ideal for curated instruction tuning. A foundational open-source alternative to proprietary RLHF data.

opc-sft-stage2.name: opc-sft-stage2
opc-sft-stage2.creator: OpenCoder
opc-sft-stage2.description: Stage 2 supervised fine-tuning dataset from the OpenCoder project. 436K examples used to train open-source code models. Covers diverse programming tasks, languages, and difficulty levels. Part of a fully open code model training pipeline.

openmathinstruct-2.name: OpenMathInstruct-2
openmathinstruct-2.creator: NVIDIA
openmathinstruct-2.description: A massive 14M-row math instruction dataset from NVIDIA, augmented from GSM8K and MATH benchmarks using Llama-3.1-405B. Covers chain-of-thought reasoning across diverse math problems at varying difficulty levels. The largest permissively licensed math reasoning dataset available for fine-tuning small models.

openmathreasoning.name: OpenMathReasoning
openmathreasoning.creator: NVIDIA
openmathreasoning.description: NVIDIA's massive 4.9M-row math reasoning dataset with chain-of-thought solutions. Covers a wide range of difficulty levels from arithmetic to competition math. Designed for teaching models to reason step-by-step through mathematical problems. Complements OpenMathInstruct-2 for comprehensive math fine-tuning.

opus-100.name: OPUS-100
opus-100.creator: Helsinki-NLP
opus-100.description: A multilingual translation corpus covering 100 languages with English-centric parallel data. 1M sentence pairs across diverse language families. Ideal for fine-tuning small models on translation tasks for low-resource language pairs.

self-oss-instruct-sc2.name: self-oss-instruct-sc2-exec-filter-50k
self-oss-instruct-sc2.creator: bigcode
self-oss-instruct-sc2.description: Self-instruction dataset from the StarCoder2 project with execution-based filtering. 50K high-quality coding problems where generated solutions were verified by actually running the code. Produced during the training of StarCoder2, one of the best open code models.

smoltalk.name: smoltalk
smoltalk.creator: HuggingFaceTB
smoltalk.description: The core instruction-tuning dataset used to train Hugging Face's SmolLM2 family of models. A carefully curated 1.1M-row mixture covering chat, reasoning, code, and general instruction following. Fully open under Apache 2.0, making it the go-to starting point for fine-tuning small language models on diverse tasks.

spider.name: Spider
spider.creator: Yale University
spider.description: The definitive benchmark dataset for text-to-SQL generation, containing 10,181 natural language questions paired with SQL queries across 200 complex databases spanning 138 domains. Features multi-table joins, nested queries, and varied SQL complexity levels. The standard evaluation benchmark for the text-to-SQL task.

sql-create-context.name: SQL-Create-Context
sql-create-context.creator: b-mc2
sql-create-context.description: A clean dataset aligning natural language questions with their corresponding SQL CREATE TABLE context and correct SQL queries. Ideal for fine-tuning small models on narrow text-to-sql translation.

squad-v2.name: SQuAD v2
squad-v2.creator: Stanford
squad-v2.description: The Stanford Question Answering Dataset v2 — the standard for reading comprehension evaluation. 150K+ questions over Wikipedia articles, including unanswerable questions that test a model's ability to know when it doesn't know. Essential for training and evaluating QA capabilities.

ultrafeedback-binarized-preferences.name: ultrafeedback-binarized-preferences-cleaned
ultrafeedback-binarized-preferences.creator: argilla
ultrafeedback-binarized-preferences.description: A cleaned and decontaminated version of the UltraFeedback preference dataset, containing 61K high-quality preference pairs scored by GPT-4. Designed for Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) to align models with human preferences. The MIT license makes it suitable for both research and commercial alignment workflows.

wikisql.name: WikiSQL
wikisql.creator: Salesforce Research
wikisql.description: The original text-to-SQL benchmark. 80K examples pairing natural language questions with SQL queries over Wikipedia tables. Simpler than Spider (single-table queries only) but larger and ideal for initial fine-tuning before moving to more complex multi-table tasks.

wildchat-1m.name: WildChat-1M
wildchat-1m.creator: Allen Institute for AI
wildchat-1m.description: Over 1M real conversations between users and GPT-3.5/GPT-4. Captures the genuine distribution of user requests — from coding to creative writing to roleplay. A critical resource for training models that handle the messy, diverse reality of user interactions.

behavioral-anomaly-detection.description: Distinguishing human-generated behavioral telemetry from autonomous agent (bot) behavior by analyzing sequences of events, inter-event timing patterns, and signal jitter characteristics. Critical for detecting AI-driven fraud, agent hijacking, and synthetic biometric generation. Requires models to identify subtle statistical regularities that separate human chaos from machine precision.
behavioral-anomaly-detection.input_format: Tokenized behavioral sequence (event types, delta-time bins, jitter bins) with max 512 tokens
behavioral-anomaly-detection.output_format: Binary classification (human vs bot) with confidence score and anomaly ranking

recommended_models.notes: Purpose-built for this task — 6.3M params, 6.5 MB INT8, F1 0.93 on blind test.

code-generation.description: The process of generating syntactically correct and semantically meaningful source code from natural language descriptions. Covers function completion, bug fixing, code translation between languages, and implementing algorithms from specifications.
code-generation.input_format: Natural language instruction or problem description, optionally with existing code context
code-generation.output_format: Source code in the requested programming language

recommended_models.notes: 71.3% HumanEval — top code performer at 4B scale with multimodal support.
Exceptional code generation across multiple languages at 7.6B.
Dual-mode reasoning ideal for code assistants, fully open for research.
Strong coding with thinking-mode toggle for complex algorithms.

extractive-qa.description: Reading a passage and extracting the exact answer span for a given question. Unlike knowledge QA (which relies on memorized facts), extractive QA tests reading comprehension — the answer must be found in the provided text. Fundational for RAG pipelines.
extractive-qa.output_format: Answer span extracted verbatim from the context

recommended_models.notes: Strong reading comprehension, ideal for RAG pipelines.
Long context support enables processing lengthy passages.
Fully open, good baseline for RAG research.

function-calling.description: The ability of a model to select, parameterize, and invoke external tools or API functions based on user intent. Includes recognizing when a tool is needed, extracting correct arguments from context, formatting calls according to a schema, and interpreting tool outputs to continue the conversation or task.
function-calling.input_format: User request + available function/tool definitions (name, description, parameter schema)
function-calling.output_format: Structured function call with function name and arguments (JSON), or final response after tool use

recommended_models.notes: Purpose-built for enterprise function calling at just 2B — remarkably efficient.
Native structured JSON output, strong agentic tool calling with huge ecosystem.
JSON/XML/Python tool calling support, dual-mode reasoning for complex tool chains.
Qwen-Agent / MCP compatible, strong for multi-tool orchestration.

general-reasoning.description: The broadest evaluation of a language model's capabilities — encompassing logical deduction, common-sense reasoning, text generation quality, and versatility across unconstrained domains. This is the baseline task that most general-purpose SLMs are evaluated against before specialization.
general-reasoning.output_format: Coherent, contextually appropriate natural language response

recommended_models.notes: The best all-around general-purpose SLM under 8B — thinking mode, multilingual, 256K context.
Gold standard 1B baseline — trained on 3T tokens with Chinchilla-optimal scaling.
Original registry seed model — lightweight reasoning champion.
Successor to Phi-3-mini with improved reasoning and knowledge recall.
Most transparent open model — ideal for reasoning research and fine-tuning.

instruction-following.description: The foundational ability of a language model to accurately understand and execute natural language instructions. Encompasses formatting compliance, multi-step task execution, constraint satisfaction, and refusal of harmful requests. This is the core competency from which specialized tasks are derived.
instruction-following.input_format: Natural language instruction with optional context, examples, and constraints
instruction-following.output_format: Response that satisfies all specified constraints and formatting requirements

recommended_models.notes: 100+ languages, thinking mode — top all-around instruction follower at 4B.
Structured output support (JSON/XML), excellent for constrained generation.
Fully open research model, strong baseline for instruction-following studies.
Massive community adoption, strong structured output, proven reliability.
Best-in-class reasoning at 3.8B, handles complex multi-step instructions well.

knowledge-qa.description: The ability to answer factual questions by drawing on knowledge encoded during pre-training. Tests a model's capacity to recall facts, reason over stored knowledge, and handle ambiguous or under-specified queries. Ranges from trivia-style factoid questions to complex multi-hop reasoning across knowledge domains.
knowledge-qa.input_format: Natural language question, optionally with retrieved context passages (RAG)

recommended_models.notes: ARC-C 83.7% — best knowledge reasoning at 3.8B.
Strong factuality across 100+ languages, long-context for RAG.
Fully open training data enables knowledge-probing research.
Original seed model of the registry — solid knowledge QA baseline.

machine-translation.description: Translating text from one language to another while preserving meaning, tone, and nuance. Tests cross-lingual understanding and generation. Critical for making AI accessible across language communities.
machine-translation.input_format: Source language text + target language specification
machine-translation.output_format: Fluent translation in the target language

recommended_models.notes: 100+ languages, state-of-the-art multilingual translation at 4B.
29+ languages with strong cross-lingual understanding.
Significantly improved multilingual over Phi-3-mini, MIT licensed.

math-reasoning.description: The ability to solve mathematical problems through step-by-step logical reasoning. Includes arithmetic, algebra, geometry, word problems, and competition-level math. Models must demonstrate chain-of-thought reasoning to arrive at correct numerical or symbolic answers.
math-reasoning.input_format: Natural language math problem, optionally with mathematical notation (LaTeX)
math-reasoning.output_format: Step-by-step reasoning chain followed by final answer

recommended_models.notes: 89.2% GSM8K — best math performer at 4B scale.
Strong mathematical reasoning with long-context for complex proofs.
Thinking mode excels at step-by-step mathematical reasoning.
Surprisingly capable for its size — good baseline for math fine-tuning experiments.

multimodal-reasoning.description: The ability to process and reason across multiple input modalities — text, images, audio, and video — to answer questions, generate descriptions, or perform tasks. Requires understanding relationships between modalities and grounding language in visual or auditory context.
multimodal-reasoning.output_format: Natural language response grounded in multimodal context

recommended_models.notes: Text + image support at 4B, strong vision-language reasoning.
Text + image + audio + video at just 2.3B effective — best for edge multimodal.

recommended_datasets.notes: Multimodal fine-tuning datasets with permissive licenses are scarce — this is a known gap in the open-source ecosystem.

named-entity-recognition.description: Identifying and classifying named entities (persons, organizations, locations, dates, etc.) in unstructured text. A foundational NLP task critical for information extraction, knowledge graph construction, and document understanding pipelines.
named-entity-recognition.output_format: Token-level entity labels (e.g., PER, ORG, LOC, MISC)

recommended_models.notes: Strong structured output, ideal for entity extraction at 2B.
Surprisingly capable at NER despite tiny size — great for lightweight extraction.
Fully open, strong at structured extraction tasks.

preference-alignment.description: The process of aligning a language model's outputs with human preferences using techniques like Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) or Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF). This is a post-training step applied after supervised fine-tuning to improve helpfulness, harmlessness, and honesty.
preference-alignment.input_format: Prompt + chosen response + rejected response (preference pairs)
preference-alignment.output_format: Aligned model that produces responses preferred by humans

recommended_datasets.notes: 61K cleaned GPT-4 scored preference pairs — the standard for DPO alignment.

recommended_models.notes: Fully open pipeline — can study alignment from base to aligned model.
Open training data enables full alignment research reproducibility.

sentiment-analysis.description: Classifying the sentiment or emotional tone of text — positive, negative, neutral, or fine-grained emotions. A bread-and-butter NLP task used in customer feedback analysis, social media monitoring, and brand intelligence. Well-suited to small, specialized models.
sentiment-analysis.output_format: Sentiment label with optional confidence score

recommended_models.notes: Tiny 135M model sufficient for sentiment classification when fine-tuned.
Minimal resource requirements, strong at text classification.
Good baseline with strong text understanding at 1.1B.

text-summarization.description: Condensing long documents, articles, or conversations into shorter summaries while preserving key information and meaning. Tests a model's ability to identify salient content, maintain factual accuracy, and produce fluent compressed text.

recommended_models.notes: 256K context ideal for long-document summarization.
128K context, MIT licensed, strong at concise generation.
Fully open, dual-mode reasoning for extractive and abstractive summarization.

text-to-sql.description: The process of translating natural language questions or instructions into valid, executable SQL queries based on a given database schema. Requires understanding of table relationships, column types, and SQL dialect nuances.
text-to-sql.input_format: Plain text question + SQL CREATE TABLE schema definitions

recommended_models.notes: Strong code reasoning and schema understanding at 7.6B.
Excellent reasoning at 3.8B, good for simple-to-moderate queries.
Strong general-purpose model with long-context support for large schemas.

models.notes: Purpose-built for this task — 6.3M params, 6.5 MB INT8, F1 0.93 on blind test.
71.3% HumanEval — top code performer at 4B scale with multimodal support.
89.2% GSM8K — best math performer at 4B scale.
Text + image support at 4B, strong vision-language reasoning.
Exceptional code generation across multiple languages at 7.6B.
29+ languages with strong cross-lingual understanding.
Strong mathematical reasoning with long-context for complex proofs.
Strong code reasoning and schema understanding at 7.6B.
Dual-mode reasoning ideal for code assistants, fully open for research.
Fully open, good baseline for RAG research.
JSON/XML/Python tool calling support, dual-mode reasoning for complex tool chains.
Most transparent open model — ideal for reasoning research and fine-tuning.
Structured output support (JSON/XML), excellent for constrained generation.
Fully open pipeline — can study alignment from base to aligned model.
Fully open, dual-mode reasoning for extractive and abstractive summarization.
Strong coding with thinking-mode toggle for complex algorithms.
Long context support enables processing lengthy passages.
Qwen-Agent / MCP compatible, strong for multi-tool orchestration.
The best all-around general-purpose SLM under 8B — thinking mode, multilingual, 256K context.
100+ languages, thinking mode — top all-around instruction follower at 4B.
Strong factuality across 100+ languages, long-context for RAG.
100+ languages, state-of-the-art multilingual translation at 4B.
Thinking mode excels at step-by-step mathematical reasoning.
256K context ideal for long-document summarization.
Strong general-purpose model with long-context support for large schemas.
Strong reading comprehension, ideal for RAG pipelines.
Successor to Phi-3-mini with improved reasoning and knowledge recall.
Best-in-class reasoning at 3.8B, handles complex multi-step instructions well.
ARC-C 83.7% — best knowledge reasoning at 3.8B.
Excellent reasoning at 3.8B, good for simple-to-moderate queries.
Purpose-built for enterprise function calling at just 2B — remarkably efficient.
Strong structured output, ideal for entity extraction at 2B.
Native structured JSON output, strong agentic tool calling with huge ecosystem.
Massive community adoption, strong structured output, proven reliability.

datasets.notes: Multimodal fine-tuning datasets with permissive licenses are scarce — this is a known gap in the open-source ecosystem.

models.notes: Gold standard 1B baseline — trained on 3T tokens with Chinchilla-optimal scaling.
Surprisingly capable for its size — good baseline for math fine-tuning experiments.
Good baseline with strong text understanding at 1.1B.
Original registry seed model — lightweight reasoning champion.
Original seed model of the registry — solid knowledge QA baseline.
Fully open research model, strong baseline for instruction-following studies.
Fully open training data enables knowledge-probing research.
Open training data enables full alignment research reproducibility.
Significantly improved multilingual over Phi-3-mini, MIT licensed.
128K context, MIT licensed, strong at concise generation.
